_meta.purpose: Per-district source table for the Nassau address feature. Every field carries a source_ref (url, vintage, verified date). A field with no verifiable primary source is omitted, never estimated. See .claude/scratch/districts-provenance.md for the full per-field fetch log.
_meta.keying: Objects are keyed by the Census school-district NAME as returned by the US Census geocoder district layers; geoid is the fallback key. sdType records which TIGER layer the district lives in: unified, elementary, or secondary.

schema.dtfName: district name as printed in the NYS DTF STAR tables
schema.starCredit: 2025 final STAR credit, Class 1 (Homestead for the two cities), dollars: {basic, enhanced}
schema.starExemptSavings: 2025 final STAR exemption savings, Class 1: {basic, enhanced}
schema.starMaxExemptSavings2026_27: DTF maximum 2026-27 STAR exemption savings, Class 1: {basic, enhanced}
schema.medianValue: ACS 2020-24 5-yr B25077 median owner-occupied home value for the place FIPS named in source_refs (a place-level proxy where the district spans multiple places; district-geography cross-check logged in source_refs)
schema.schoolRate: school tax rate per $100 of county assessed value, from one representative in-district Nassau LRV parcel; tax year 2026 = school year 2025-26
schema.libraryRate: library tax rate per $100 county AV from the same LRV parcel
schema.levy: school district tax levy, dollars, fiscal year ending 2025 = 2024-25 school year (NYS OSC via data.ny.gov iq85-sdzs); multi-municipality districts are the sum of their segment rows
schema.enrollment: {cur: 2024-25 K-12, base: 2015-16 K-12} per NYSED data site (BEDS day counts)

_meta.coverage: Full rows: 3 pilot districts (Rockville Centre UFSD, Long Beach City SD, Locust Valley CSD). All other DTF-listed Nassau districts carry STAR-only stub rows pending the fan-out.
notes: DTF lists 55 taxing school districts for Nassau; the Nassau BOCES directory counts 56 districts. The three central high school districts (Bellmore-Merrick, Sewanhaka, Valley Stream CHSD) do not appear as separate DTF STAR rows: their levies ride on their component elementary districts’ bills. Addresses in those areas resolve through the Census elementary + secondary layers, not the unified layer.

Amityville and Cold Spring Harbor are Suffolk-home-county districts with Nassau territory; their 2025 STAR rows here come from the DTF Nassau page, but DTF publishes their 2026-27 max exemption savings on the Suffolk page (sd52), which is out of scope, so that field is omitted for them.
The DTF max-savings page spells one district “Floral Park-Bellrose”; normalized here to the comparison-table spelling “Floral Park-Bellerose”.
FOIL-gated §467 aggregates (parcel counts, exemption-gap dollars) are intentionally absent from this file on every framing.

Long Beach City SD schoolRate scope: the logged rate is the COUNTY-roll rate paid by the district’s Town of Hempstead portion (Lido Beach / Point Lookout). The City of Long Beach assesses its own roll; a city-parcel rate on the city AV basis is not yet verified and is omitted.

county_leg is present only where the repo’s same-day-verified officials table covers it (RVC, LD-1). Other districts’ county legislators are an officials-lane task, re-verified same-day before any politician-facing use.

Rockville Centre Union Free School Distr.censusName: Rockville Centre Union Free School District

schoolRate.vintage: tax year 2026 (school 2025-26); Net School Tax 2247.867 + Net Library 84.459 per $100 on AV 653, Combined School Taxes $15,230.09
schoolRate.note: same in-village parcel as ledger row #f-rates; re-fetched and matched to the penny

levy.crosscheck: ledger #f-budget26: 2025-26 levy $109.24M = +2.6% on this figure, exact

Long Beach City School District.schoolRateScope: county-roll (Town of Hempstead portion: Lido Beach / Point Lookout); City of Long Beach parcels are on the city’s own assessment roll and are not covered by this rate
Long Beach City School District.town: City of Long Beach (plus Lido Beach and Point Lookout in Town of Hempstead)

medianValue.vintage: ACS 2020-24 5-yr, Long Beach city (place 3643335); place is a proxy: the district also covers Lido Beach and Point Lookout

schoolRate.vintage: tax year 2026 (school 2025-26); Net School Tax 1238.265 + Net Library 44.782 per $100 on AV 580; parcel 16 Harrogate St, Lido Beach, class 210.01 single-family
schoolRate.note: parcel’s Basic STAR line −$613.00 independently matches the DTF Basic exemption savings for this district

levy.vintage: FY ending 2025 (2024-25); sum of segment rows: city roll $84,062,477 + Town of Hempstead $25,018,062
levy.crosscheck: LI Herald reported the adopted 2024-25 levy as $109.1M (liherald.com voters-pass-2024-25-city-school-district-budget,208000; page 403s automated fetch, figure from search-result text 2026-07-20)

medianValue.vintage: ACS 2020-24 5-yr, Locust Valley CDP (place 3643192); place is a proxy: the district also covers Bayville, Lattingtown, Matinecock and Mill Neck

schoolRate.vintage: tax year 2026 (school 2025-26); Net School Tax 1626.547 + Net Library 68.394 per $100 on AV 758; parcel 35 Weir Ln, Locust Valley, class 210.01 single-family
schoolRate.note: second in-district parcel 30021++00170 (18 Locust Pl) shows the identical 1626.547 rate

levy.vintage: FY ending 2025 (2024-25); single Oyster Bay segment

enrollment.note: −16.0% since 2015-16; the site’s “15% drop” Locust Valley claim is consistent
